content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第三部分。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Section 3.

content.cn_text: 你将听到两位学生，马克和弗兰，讨论他们关于一种独特的新西兰海豚的演讲。
content.en_text: You will hear a discussion between two students called Mark and Fran about their presentation

content.cn_text: 关于一些新西兰特有的海豚。
content.en_text: on some dolphins which are unique to New Zealand.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第5页的问题21至24。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在仔细听并回答问题21至24。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 嗨，马克，谢谢你过来。
content.en_text: [Fran]: Hi Mark, thanks for coming around.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 嗨，弗兰，没问题。
content.en_text: [Mark]: Hi Fran, no problem.

content.cn_text: 我们需要努力完成这个演讲。
content.en_text: We need to try and get this presentation finished.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 马克，我们从海豚发声的方式开始吧。
content.en_text: [Fran]: Mark, let's start with the way dolphins make sounds.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 嗯，我们都知道海豚会发出咔嗒声来交流。
content.en_text: [Mark]: Well we all know that dolphins make clicking noises to communicate.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 是的，它们和人类一样有舌头和喉咙，但海豚真正奇怪的地方在于
content.en_text: [Fran]: Yes, they have tongues and larynxes as humans do, but what's really strange about the dolphin

content.cn_text: 声音是通过一个叫做“millen”的奇特器官聚焦的，
content.en_text: is that the sounds are focused through an extraordinary organ called the millen,

content.cn_text: 这是大多数其他海洋哺乳动物所没有的。
content.en_text: which most other sea mammals don't have.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 那我从这里接下去讲不同类型的海豚吗？
content.en_text: [Mark]: So shall I take over from there and speak about the different dolphin types?

content.cn_text: 我特别想分享我们发现的关于那些新西兰特有的稀有海豚的信息。
content.en_text: I want to share what we found out about those rare dolphins that are special just to New Zealand in particular.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 是的，太好了，把重点放在它们身上。
content.en_text: [Fran]: Yeah, great, put the emphasis on them.

content.cn_text: 最稀有的是赫氏海豚，不是吗？
content.en_text: The rarest of all is the hectares dolphin, isn't it?

content.cn_text: 它们正处于危险之中，不是吗？
content.en_text: They are in danger, aren't they?

content.cn_text: 我认为那主要是因为它们中有太多被渔具缠住，
content.en_text: I think that's mainly because so many of them get tangled up in fishing gear,

content.cn_text: 尤其是刺网缠住并死亡。
content.en_text: especially girl nets and die.

content.cn_text: 我知道一些环保人士担心旅游业对海豚数量的影响，
content.en_text: I know that some conservationists are worried about the impact of the tourism industry on dolphin numbers,

content.cn_text: 但我不认为这在这里还是个问题。
content.en_text: but I don't think that's a problem yet here.

content.cn_text: 另外，记住这个物种的繁殖周期非常长，
content.en_text: Also, remember that this species has a very long reproductive cycle,

content.cn_text: 所以它们的数量不会很快得到补充。
content.en_text: so their numbers don't get replenished quickly.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 是的，这真令人难过。
content.en_text: [Mark]:: Yeah, that's really sad.

content.cn_text: 但至少新西兰政府为它们建立了一个保护区，不是吗？
content.en_text: But at least the New Zealand government established a sanctuary for them, didn't it?

content.cn_text: 渔业反对它，但我记得读到公众
content.en_text: The fishing industry was against it, but I remember reading that the general public

content.cn_text: 非常支持这个决定。
content.en_text: were very supportive of the decision.

content.cn_text: 这是海洋生物学家进行的一项研究的结果。
content.en_text: It was a consequence of a study done by some marine biologists.

content.cn_text: 拥有一个受保护的繁殖区已经对海豚数量产生了很大影响。
content.en_text: Having a protected breeding area has made a big difference to the dolphin population already.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 是的，我认为它们的总数已经增加到7000多只，
content.en_text: [Fran]: Yeah, I think their numbers have swelled to over 7,000 in total,

content.cn_text: 其中只有89只的小群体生活在南地附近，
content.en_text: with a tiny group of just 89 living down off Southland,

content.cn_text: 近2000只生活在基督城附近的东海岸，
content.en_text: nearly 2,000 living near Christchurch on the East Coast

content.cn_text: 而最大的群体生活在西海岸。
content.en_text: and the biggest groups surviving off the West Coast.

content.cn_text: 那里是相当荒野的地区，所以我想没有那么多游客和渔民打扰它们。
content.en_text: It's pretty wild country over there, so I guess there aren't as many tourists and fishermen to disturb them.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 在你听到讨论的其余部分之前，
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Before you hear the rest of the discussion,

content.cn_text: 你有一些时间查看第6页的问题25至30。
content.en_text: you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听并回答问题25至30。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 好的，你建议我如何安排我这部分的演讲？
content.en_text: [Mark]: Okay, so how do you suggest I structure my part of the talk?

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 嗯，你可以从普通海豚开始，
content.en_text: [Fran]: Well, you could start with the common dolphin,

content.cn_text: 它们栖息在新西兰周围的整个海域。
content.en_text: which inhabits the whole of the sea around New Zealand.

content.cn_text: 一旦你涵盖了所有与它们相关的内容，比如捕食等等，
content.en_text: And once you've covered everything to do with them, feeding and so on,

content.cn_text: 你就可以继续讲更不寻常的类型。
content.en_text: you go on to the more unusual types.

content.cn_text: 斑纹海豚特别有趣，因为你知道所有的海豚都以被称为“群”的小社会群体旅行吗？
content.en_text: The dusky dolphin is especially interesting because you know how all the dolphins travel in small social groups called pods?

content.cn_text: 嗯，斑纹海豚群的成员数量多达800只。
content.en_text: Well, the dusky dolphins travel in pods as big as 800 members.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 我认为斑纹海豚喜欢跟在渔船和旅游船旁边旅行，不是吗？
content.en_text: [Mark]: And I think the duskies like to travel along beside fishing and tourist boats, don't they?

content.cn_text: 而赫氏海豚则不同，它们喜欢跟在船只的尾流中，
content.en_text: Whereas the hectares dolphins are different in that they prefer to go in the wake of boats,

content.cn_text: 也就是说，跟在后面。
content.en_text: that is, coming along in the rear.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 是的，它们会的。但它们也会潜入深处以避开快速行驶的船只。
content.en_text: [Fran]: Yeah, they do. But they also dive down deep to avoid fast boats.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 哦，别忘了毛伊海豚，它们也濒临灭绝，不是吗？
content.en_text: [Mark]: Oh, let's not forget the Maui because they are endangered too aren't they?

content.cn_text: 它们只剩下150只，并且只栖息在新西兰北岛的西部沿海水域。
content.en_text: There are only 150 of them left and they inhabit only the western coastal waters of the North Island of New Zealand.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 是的，真可惜。它们也因为体型小而面临问题。
content.en_text: [Fran]: Yeah, it's a pity. They also face problems because of their tiny size.

content.cn_text: 虎鲸实际上也是一种海豚，对吗？
content.en_text: Now the Orca is actually a dolphin too, right?

content.cn_text: [马克]: 是的，令人惊讶。所以我会把它纳入我的演讲。
content.en_text: [Mark]: Yes, surprisingly. So I'll include that in my talk.

content.cn_text: 人们称它们为虎鲸，但实际上它们不是鲸鱼。
content.en_text: People call them killer whales, but in fact they're not whales.

content.cn_text: 它们是一种巨大而凶猛的海豚。
content.en_text: They're a huge and very fierce type of dolphin.

content.cn_text: 在新西兰，南岛的凯库拉是发现虎鲸游过的最佳地点。
content.en_text: In New Zealand, Kaikura in the South Island is the best place to spot Orca swimming by.

content.cn_text: 所以人们错误地认为它们就生活在那里。
content.en_text: So people mistakenly think that's where they live.

content.cn_text: 但实际上虎鲸会游很远的距离。
content.en_text: But actually Orca's cover vast distances.

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 别忘了提瓶鼻海豚。
content.en_text: [Fran]: Let's not forget to mention the bottle nose.

content.cn_text: 它比几乎所有其他海豚都大，甚至比普通海豚也大。
content.en_text: It's bigger than nearly all the other dolphins, even the common dolphin.

content.cn_text: [马克]: 哦，但还是没有虎鲸大，对吗？
content.en_text: [Mark]: Oh, but still not as big as the Orca, right?

content.cn_text: [弗兰]: 是的。
content.en_text: [Fran]: That's right.

content.cn_text: 嗯，我想我们的演讲现在完成了。
content.en_text: Well, I think that's our presentation done now.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: That is the end of section three.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在翻到第七页的第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section four on page seven.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

items.title: What is the students' purpose in mentioning the way dolphins make sounds?

options.title: A
options.content: to show comparison with human speech

options.title: B
options.content: to show the unusual anatomy of a dolphin's head

options.title: C
options.content: to show recent developments in understanding the meaning of the sounds

items.title: According to Fran, Hector's dolphins are unaffected by
The New Zealand Government set up a marine sanctuary

options.title: B
options.content: as a result of scientific research.

options.title: C
options.content: to protect a dolphin feeding ground.

items.title: Where is the largest population of Hector's dolphins in New Zealand?

groups.desc: Write the correct letter, A–H, next to each question.
groups.title: Questions 25-30.

list.title: C
list.content: live along New Zealand's entire coastline

list.title: F
list.content: live only off New Zealand's northwest coast

list.title: G
list.content: are the second largest dolphin species in New Zealand
